_comment: OSRS-style 'Use X with Y' recipes for eldermoor_client.html. Consumed by src/inventory-ops.js's use-mode resolver. Two shapes: item+item pairs (a,b are both inventory item ids) and item+fixture pairs (a is an item id, fixture is a scenery/fixture verb such as 'Cook'/'Smelt'/'Smith' that world.js already assigns to fire/range/furnace/anvil nodes). Order of a/b (or item/fixture) does not matter when resolving - the engine tries both directions.

itemPairs.message: You mix the flour and water into a soft dough.

itemFixture.message: You attempt to light the logs.
You attempt to light the logs.
itemFixture.note: Fallback: lighting logs does not strictly require clicking an existing fire (there is none yet the first time), so tinderbox+logs also resolves as a plain item-on-item use.

itemFixture.message: You cook the shrimp on the fire.
You bake the dough into a warm loaf of bread.
You place the ore into the furnace.
You place the ore into the furnace.
You hammer the bar on the anvil.
